name: Session Intelligence + Project Memory
description: Make Tide the agent that remembers. Persistent session summaries, project memory store, smart context injection that makes every conversation benefit from past work, session history UI, and a project dashboard.
notes: Session summaries stored as markdown in .tide/sessions/<timestamp>.md.
Project memory is a key-value store in .tide/memory.json accessible via tide_memory tool.
Smart context injection uses token budgeting to select what to inject.
Can develop in parallel with Phases 8 and 9.
This phase transforms Tide from session-based to project-aware.

tasks.title: Build Session Summary Generation
tasks.description: At the end of each agent session (or on manual trigger via Command Palette), generate a summary: files changed, decisions made, tools used, remaining TODOs. Store in .tide/sessions/<timestamp>.md. Use a fast model for summarization to minimize cost. New Pi extension tide-session.ts or extend tide-project.ts.

tasks.title: Build Project Memory Store
tasks.description: .tide/memory.json: key-value store of project facts the agent has learned (architecture decisions, naming conventions, test patterns, dependency choices). Register tide_memory custom tool in tide-project.ts: read_memory(key?), write_memory(key, value), list_memories(). Agent can read and write memory during sessions.

tasks.title: Implement Smart Context Injection
tasks.description: Enhance tide-project.ts before_agent_start hook with priority-based injection: (1) TIDE.md rules (always, never trimmable), (2) pinned region tags (always), (3) active feature plan (if exists), (4) repo map summary (if exists, trimmable), (5) last 2-3 session summaries (trimmable), (6) relevant memory entries matching prompt keywords (trimmable). Token budget estimation to stay within model context limits.

tasks.title: Build Session History UI
tasks.description: New panel or tab showing past sessions from .tide/sessions/. Each entry: timestamp, summary excerpt, files changed count. Click to expand full summary. 'Continue from session' button that loads the summary as context for a new conversation. Reads session files via Rust FS commands.

tasks.title: Build Project Dashboard
tasks.description: Replace the 'Open a folder to get started' welcome screen with a project dashboard. Shows: recent workspaces (with last opened date), active feature plans (from .tide/features/), recent session activity, context budget overview, quick actions (Open Folder, New Session, Run Tests). Makes Tide feel like a project management tool, not just an editor.
